time_blocks.summary: Content Scheduling & Prep for the Week
time_blocks.description:
Prep all content for the upcoming week:
- Review content calendar template
- Plan filming shot lists for Tuesday and Thursday
- Research trending topics in your niches (fitness + AI)
- Queue any ready content for publishing

This is Week 1. First filming day is Tuesday. Topic: "I Have Brain Cancer. Here's Why I Still Train Every Day."

Humiston formula: Problem → Science → Solution → Demo. 10 minutes. No fluff.

Film 2-3 YouTube videos in one session. Batch = efficiency.

Your first 10 topics:
1. I Have Brain Cancer. Here's Why I Still Train Every Day.
2. Training With Dystonia — 5 Exercises That Actually Work
3. The Peptide Protocol That Changed My Recovery
4. How to Train When Your Body Fights You
5. GH Secretagogues Explained
6. Full Workout for People With Neurological Conditions
7. Why Most Fitness Advice Fails Chronically Ill People
8. My Cancer Diagnosis Changed How I Program Workouts
9. 5 Exercises Every Person With a Movement Disorder Should Know
10. The Honest Truth About Training Through Chemotherapy

Also create 3-5 Shorts/Reels from each long-form video.

Post-film production block:
- Edit YouTube videos (use AI video tools in media tower)
- Write titles using Humiston formulas: Problem/Solution, Curiosity Gap, Contrarian Hook, Direct Promise
- Create thumbnails: bold text, your physique, high contrast
- Schedule posts across YouTube, Instagram, TikTok
- Cut 3-5 Shorts/Reels per long-form video

Humiston rule: Consistency beats perfection. 2 good videos/week > 1 perfect video/month.

Check all channels for replies from morning outreach blitz:
- Text messages, DMs, LinkedIn, email
- Respond within 5 minutes (Hormozi: speed to lead wins)
- Book discovery calls immediately — don't let warm leads cool
- Send Loom demos to anyone showing interest
- Log all conversations in CRM

Hormozi: The fortune is in the follow-up. Most people give up after 1 message. You follow up 5-7 times.

Build your prospect machine:
- Apollo.io: Search Naples businesses, 5-50 employees
- Target niches: home services, med spas, dental, real estate, restaurants
- Build segmented lists by industry
- Write/refine 3-email cold sequence:
  Email 1: Problem + proof ("I built a system for [similar biz] that captured 23 leads in month 1")
  Email 2 (day 3): Loom demo + cost comparison
  Email 3 (day 7): Scarcity close ("Taking 2 more clients this month")
- Load into Instantly.ai
- Target: 50-100 cold emails/day

Saraev data: 1-3% reply rate = 1-3 meetings/day at 100 emails.

Saraev started by hitting 50-100 businesses per day door-to-door. You're doing 5. That's manageable.

Bring:
- Your AI services one-pager (branded PDF)
- Business cards
- Phone ready to show a 2-min Loom demo of your live systems

Script: "Hi, I'm William with Marceau Solutions. I help local businesses automate their lead follow-up so they never miss a customer. I built a system that [specific result]. Can I show you a 2-minute demo?"

Target: HVAC companies, med spas, dental offices, restaurants, real estate offices in Naples.

Even if they say no, ask: "Who do you know that might benefit from this?"

For any prospects showing interest from outreach:
- Record personalized 2-min Loom showing THEIR specific pain point
- Generate branded PDF proposal using proposal template
- Include: their problem, your solution, timeline (7 days), pricing, guarantee

For prospects not yet engaged:
- Record generic Loom demos of your systems to attach to cold outreach
- Build library of demo videos by industry (HVAC demo, restaurant demo, etc.)

Saraev: A personalized Loom converts better than any email.

Follow up on EVERY open conversation from the week:
- Check all DMs, texts, emails for responses
- Send follow-up #2, #3, #4 to non-responders
- Hormozi: Most people quit after 1 message. You send 5-7.
- Log all interactions in CRM/Google Sheets
- Move warm leads to discovery call stage
- Send relevant Loom demos to warming prospects

The pipeline is: Outreach → Reply → Loom Demo → Discovery Call → Proposal → Close
This block moves people through that pipeline.

1 hour focused study on overcoming objections:
- "I can't afford it" → Hormozi reframe: cost of NOT doing it
- "I need to think about it" → "What specifically do you need to think about?"
- "I already have someone" → "How's that working for you?"
- "Send me more info" → "Happy to — what specifically would help you decide?"

Practice scripts out loud. Role-play mentally. This hour feeds directly into tomorrow's outreach.

Second filming day of the week. By Thursday you've had 2 outreach-heavy days — use this creative block while the energy shifts.

- Film 1-2 YouTube long-form videos
- Film 5+ Shorts/Reels (quick tips, exercise demos, peptide explainers)
- Film at gym if possible — real environment > studio
- Record B-roll of your actual training for future content

Humiston production style: Good lighting, gym setting, teach while demonstrating. The production serves the content, not the other way around.

Process all content from this morning's filming:
- Edit 1-2 YouTube videos
- Create thumbnails (bold text, physique, high contrast)
- Write SEO-optimized titles and descriptions
- Schedule YouTube uploads
- Cut and schedule Shorts/Reels across IG, TikTok, YouTube Shorts
- Engage with comments on recently posted videos

Target: 2 long-form + 4-5 Shorts published per week minimum.

Build the fitness revenue engine:

Week 1-2: Create "Defy the Odds" 90-day group coaching offer
- Program structure, weekly schedule, deliverables
- Pricing: $97/mo
- Landing page copy
- Hormozi guarantee: "If you don't see measurable progress in 30 days, I'll coach you 1:1 for free until you do"

Week 3-4: Create $19.99 digital program (Humiston model)
- "The Defy the Odds Training System" — 30-day program for people training through health challenges
- Host on Gumroad or Thinkific

Week 5+: Build email lead magnet PDF for list building

Friday is a medium-energy day. 50 outreach actions instead of 100.

- 25 warm (DMs, texts, LinkedIn messages)
- 25 cold (emails via Instantly.ai)
- Focus on QUALITY follow-ups to the week's best leads
- Book any remaining discovery calls for next week

Hormozi: Volume on hard days, precision on lighter days. Both matter.

Fill out the weekly scorecard from the Execution Plan:

AI SERVICES:
- Outreach messages sent this week: ___ (target: 500)
- Meetings booked: ___ (target: 3-5)
- Proposals sent: ___ (target: 2-3)
- Clients closed: ___ (target: 1)
- MRR: $___

FITNESS CONTENT:
- Videos published: ___ (target: 2 long-form)
- Shorts published: ___ (target: 4-5)
- Subscribers: ___
- Email list size: ___

HEALTH:
- Training sessions: ___ (target: 4-5)
- Treatment days honored: ___
- Average energy level: ___

Review what worked. Cut what didn't. Adjust next week.

Dedicated delivery block:
- Build/refine client systems for any active or beta clients
- Package new automation templates for sales demos
- Create industry-specific Loom demo library
- Prep case studies from beta client results

If no clients yet: Build the portfolio.
- Set up a demo instance for each target niche
- Record walkthrough Looms
- Create before/after screenshots

Saraev: Your business IS the demo. Show prospects YOUR systems running.

CEO block. Step back and think strategically:

1. Review scorecard numbers
2. What was the #1 bottleneck this week?
3. What's the ONE thing to fix next week?
4. Plan next week's outreach targets
5. Schedule any discovery calls
6. Plan content topics for next filming days
7. Assign energy levels to next week's days based on treatment schedule

Hormozi principle: "What is the ONE thing blocking you from client #1? Work on THAT and only that."

Update the plan. Adjust. Never stop moving forward.

1 hour only. Deep expertise building:
- Tesamorelin, GH Secretagogues, BPC-157 protocols
- Latest research papers
- Client safety considerations
- Build content ideas from research findings

Rule: Every research session produces at least 1 content topic or 1 client-ready protocol. Research without output is procrastination disguised as work.

100 minutes of cold calls to Naples area businesses. No email, no DMs — phones only.

--- BEFORE YOU START ---

• Script ready: "Hi [Name], this is William Marceau — I work with [industry] businesses here in Naples to automate their customer follow-up and scheduling using AI. Do you have 90 seconds?"
• Timer running: track actual talk time (not dial time)

--- CALL STRUCTURE (per call, 3-5 min) ---
1. Intro (15 sec): Name, who you help, why you're calling
2. Permission (5 sec): "Do you have 90 seconds?"
3. Pain hook (30 sec): "Most [HVAC/med spa/etc] owners I talk to lose 5-10 hrs/week on [follow-up/scheduling/reviews]. That resonating?"
4. CTA (10 sec): "I'd love to show you what that looks like for your business — 15-min call this week?"
5. Log result immediately after each call

--- TRACKING ---

Categories: No answer, Voicemail, Not interested, Callback scheduled, Meeting booked

--- HOW TO GET PHONE NUMBERS ---
For leads missing phones: Google the business name → get number from Google Business Profile
Focus on businesses with 2-20 employees (owners answer their own phones)

time_blocks.summary: Sales Readiness — Pitch + Objection Handling + Discovery Call Dry Run
time_blocks.description:
Full sales practice block. No shortcuts. Work through all three sections.

--- SECTION 1: PITCH REPS (20 min) ---
• 60-second pitch × 5 reps (record at least 1 on phone, watch it back)
• 30-second version × 3 reps
• Evaluate: Does it sound natural? Would YOU be curious if a stranger said this to you?

--- SECTION 2: OBJECTION HANDLING (30 min) ---
Practice responses OUT LOUD for each:
• "We already use [software]." → Positioning: you extend what they have, not replace it
• "I can't afford it right now." → Reframe ROI — cost of NOT automating
• "I need to talk to my partner/manager." → Discovery: what would they need to see?
• "We tried something like this before and it didn't work." → Empathy + differentiation
• "How is this different from ChatGPT?" → Custom-built vs. generic, integrated vs. bolt-on
• "I don't have time to learn new tech." → You handle implementation, they see results

--- SECTION 3: DISCOVERY CALL DRY RUN (40 min) ---
Run a full simulated 30-min discovery call out loud. Use this structure:
1. Open (2 min): Thank them, confirm time, set agenda
2. Rapport (3 min): How's business lately? What are you focused on this quarter?
3. Pain discovery (10 min): Where are you losing the most time? What's the #1 thing you wish ran itself?
4. Qualify (5 min): Team size? Decision-maker? Budget range? Timeline?
5. Demo/fit (7 min): Walk through 1-2 relevant use cases for THEIR business type
6. Close (3 min): "Based on what you've shared, here's what I'd recommend... Does that make sense as a starting point?"

--- REVIEW (20 min) ---
• What felt natural? What felt forced?
• Which objections need more work?
• Update leave-behind if any messaging missed
